text.title: Prometheus Monitoring Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Prometheus Monitoring methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Prefer Histogram over Summary for latency metrics in distributed systems. Use _seconds suffix and explicit bucket boundaries.
rule.rationale: Histograms are aggregatable across replicas with sum(rate(...)) by (le). Summary quantiles are per-instance and cannot be meaningfully combined.

rule.statement: Follow namespace_name_unit_suffix in snake_case. Suffix counters with _total; histograms with _seconds or _bytes.
rule.rationale: Consistent naming allows Grafana + alerting to auto-discover metrics and lets teams understand purpose without docs.

rule.statement: Never use unbounded values as labels: no user IDs, request IDs, email addresses, or UUIDs. Each unique combination creates a new time series.
rule.rationale: 1M users × 10 endpoints = 10M time series from a single metric. Cardinality blowup causes Prometheus OOM.

rule.statement: Keep label count per metric ≤10. Add only labels needed for meaningful aggregation or filtering in dashboards and alerts.
rule.rationale: Each additional label multiplies time series exponentially. Unused labels waste memory and slow scrapes.

rule.statement: Set global scrape interval to 15s. Override to 10s only for critical systems. Use 30–60s for low-priority metrics.
rule.rationale: 10s scrape doubles storage and CPU vs 15s with minimal benefit. <10s is unsupported in practice.

rule.statement: Alert on symptoms (error rate, latency, saturation), not causes (pod crashed, OOM). Every alert MUST carry a runbook_url annotation.

rationale.source: Google SRE Workbook — Alerting on SLOs

rule.rationale: Cause-based alerts page on pod restarts even when no user impact. Symptom-based alerts describe customer pain.

rule.statement: Use range vectors with rate() for counters. Never use a counter's instant vector value directly — it resets to zero on process restart.
rule.rationale: rate(counter[5m]) handles resets automatically; raw counter values show meaningless sawtooth graphs.
